L1.text:
Chapter 1: The Hidden Light in the Stone

The wind is strong in Great Waste City. It has a lot of sand.

There is a mine far from the city. Many people work there. They dig for magic stones. It is a very sad place.

Lin Fan is sixteen years old. He is thin but strong. He is covered in sweat and black dust. He has worked in this dark mine for three years.

In this world, some people are "Immortals." They can fly and live forever. But Lin Fan is a normal boy. He is poor. He wanted to be an Immortal. But his body is "broken." He cannot feel magic. This makes him very sad.

"I must find a magic stone today," Lin Fan says. "If not, I will have no dinner." He hits the wall with his heavy tool.

Crack!

The wall breaks. Lin Fan is happy. He looks into the hole. But there is no bright stone. He only sees a small, grey stone ball. It is round and dirty. It looks like a normal rock.

Lin Fan picks it up. "Is this it?" he asks. He is disappointed.

Then, something happens. The wall is sharp. It cuts Lin Fan's finger. Red blood falls on the grey stone ball.

Suddenly, the stone ball drinks the blood.

"Ouch!" Lin Fan feels a sharp pain.

Something cold moves into his finger. It feels like a small snake. It moves fast inside his body. It goes to his heart and his arms.

Lin Fan shakes. His body feels very cold and then very warm. He remembers a secret book. It is about magic.

"Is this magic energy?" he thinks. "It is so pure!"

He sits down quickly. He closes his eyes. He breathes slowly. He moves the energy inside his body.

Outside, he looks like a poor worker. But inside, the stone ball is glowing. It is near his stomach. It has a soft light.

That night, the dark mine is cold. But the boy's body has a gold light. He is not a "broken" boy anymore. Everything is changing.

quiz.question: Where does Lin Fan work?
What does Lin Fan find in the wall?
What makes the stone ball wake up?

L2.text:
The wind in Great Waste City was always full of sand.

Fifty miles outside the city, there was a mine belonging to the Qingyun Sect. This place was like a deep scar on the earth. It was a place where many poor people lost their dreams. In the dark tunnels, the sound of heavy tools hitting rocks never stopped.

Lin Fan was sixteen years old. He worked without a shirt. His back was thin but strong. It was covered in sweat and black dust. He looked like he had black ink on his skin. Lin Fan had worked in this dark mine for three years.

In this world, "Cultivators" were very powerful. They could fly on swords and live for a long time. But normal people were like grass. They were weak. Lin Fan once dreamed of being a Cultivator. But when he was ten, a teacher tested him. The teacher said Lin Fan had "Broken Veins."

"Broken Veins" meant his body was closed. He could not feel the magic energy of the world. In the world of Cultivators, this was like a death sentence.

"Dammit! If I don't find a spirit stone today, I won't have any dinner," Lin Fan whispered. He wiped the sweat from his eyes. He held his heavy tool and hit the rock wall again.

Crack!

This time, the sound was different. The hard rock broke, and a large hole appeared.

Lin Fan was excited. He thought he found a high-quality spirit stone. However, when he looked closer, there was no bright light. He only found a round stone bead. It was about the size of a thumb. It was dark and rough. It looked like a common rock.

Lin Fan picked it up. "I worked so hard just for this?" he said. He felt very disappointed.

Suddenly, something strange happened. The tunnel was dark, and the rocks were sharp. When Lin Fan picked up the bead, a sharp rock cut his finger. Red blood fell onto the grey stone bead.

The dark bead quickly drank the blood.

"Hiss!" Lin Fan felt a sharp pain in his finger.

Then, a cold energy entered his body through the cut. It moved like a small snake. It ran into his dry veins that had been closed for sixteen years.

Lin Fan's body shook. He felt a cold feeling inside his heart. He remembered a secret magic book he once heard about.

"Is this... spirit energy? No, it is cleaner than that!"

He knew this was a big chance. Lin Fan sat down on the ground quickly. He held his breath and guided the energy to move through his body.

To other people, he was just a poor worker being lazy. But inside his body, the stone bead was floating near his stomach. It gave off a soft light.

That night, the mine was dark and cold. But the boy's "Broken Veins" began to shine with a gold light. His life was about to change forever.

quiz.question: What does "Broken Veins" mean for Lin Fan?
options: His body cannot feel or use magic energy.
He is the strongest worker in the mine.

quiz.question: How did Lin Fan feel when he first saw the stone bead?
options: He was very excited because it was bright.
He was disappointed because it looked like a normal rock.
He was scared because it was too large.

quiz.question: What happened after Lin Fan's blood touched the stone bead?
options: The stone bead broke into many pieces.
A cold and pure energy entered his body.
The tunnel started to fall down.

quiz.question: Why did Lin Fan sit down and hold his breath at the end?
options: Because he was too tired to work.
Because he wanted to hide from the teacher.
Because he wanted to guide the magic energy inside him.

L3.text:
Chapter 1: The Hidden Light in the Stone (Level 3)

The wind in Great Waste City was relentless, always carrying sharp grains of sand.

Fifty miles outside the city stood the Qingyun Sect's mine. This place was the deepest scar on the land—a place where the dreams of countless ordinary people were shattered. Inside the deep, gloomy tunnels, the dull thuds of mining tools echoed constantly, sounding like the desperate struggles of the dying.

Lin Fan was sixteen years old. Working shirtless, his thin but muscular back was drenched in sweat and covered in black dust, appearing like jagged ink marks. He was supposed to be at an age of energy and hope, yet he had already spent three long years in this dark pit.

In this world, "Cultivators" lived high above the rest. They could move mountains, dry up seas, and live forever. In contrast, normal people were as cheap as grass. Lin Fan had once dreamed of possessing a "Spirit Root" so he could fly on a sword among the clouds. However, reality was cruel. At the age of ten, during the sect's official test, he was diagnosed with "Withered Veins."

Having Withered Veins meant his meridians were naturally blocked, making it impossible for him to sense spiritual energy. In the world of cultivation, this was a death sentence for his future.

"Dammit! If I don't find at least one low-grade spirit stone today, they will cut my dinner portions again," Lin Fan muttered under his breath. He wiped the sweat from his eyes, gripped his heavy iron pickaxe, and struck the rock wall with all his might.

Crack!

The feedback from the wall was different this time. Along with a crisp cracking sound, a large fissure opened in the thick layer of rock.

Lin Fan felt a surge of joy, thinking he had finally struck a high-quality spirit stone. But as he leaned closer, there was no brilliant glow. Instead, he discovered a perfectly round stone bead. It was roughly the size of a thumb, dull in color, and had a rough surface. It looked exactly like the common rocks scattered throughout the tunnel.

Disappointed, Lin Fan reached out to pick it up. "I worked all day just for this useless thing?"

At that moment, a sudden change occurred. The light in the tunnel was dim, and the rocks were sharp. As Lin Fan grabbed the bead, his finger was accidentally sliced by a jagged edge. A drop of bright red blood slid down his finger and instantly soaked into the ordinary-looking stone bead.

The dull bead suddenly produced a strange suction the moment it touched the blood.

"Hiss!" Lin Fan gasped as he felt a sharp, stinging pain. Before he could react, a faint but extremely pure and cool stream of light surged from the wound. Like a tiny silver snake, it bolted into his fingertips.

This energy was completely different from the dusty air in the mine. Once inside his body, it raced toward his meridians—the paths that had been dry and blocked for sixteen years.

Lin Fan's body trembled violently. He felt an unprecedented coolness explode through his entire frame. His pupils narrowed as he recalled the Qi Induction Manual, a secret text he had once overheard outside the sect.

"Is this... spiritual energy? No, it's even purer than that!"

He knew this opportunity would disappear in a flash. Ignoring his body's shaking, he immediately sat cross-legged on the ground. Holding his breath, he forced himself to guide that pure energy to flow through his meridians.

To an outsider, he looked like a lowly miner being lazy. But in Lin Fan's inner vision, the stone bead was now floating at the center of his body, emitting a faint but steady glow.

That night, inside the dark and freezing mine, the boy's "hopeless" veins began to glow with a divine golden light. The stone had hidden its light, but now, the gears of fate had completely changed direction.

quiz.question: What does the term "Dragon Gate" symbolize in the text?
options: A literal gate made of dragons.
A rare opportunity to change one's destiny.

quiz.question: What was the hidden change in Lin Fan's body?
options: The stone bead was strengthening his meridians.

quiz.question: What enabled Lin Fan to dodge the fireball so easily?
options: The stone bead gave him a "spiritual sense" that slowed down his perception of the attack.

quiz.question: What happened to Wang Qian's earth shield?
options: It blocked the water arrow successfully.
It was easily pierced by Lin Fan's high-purity water arrow.

quiz.question: How did the other disciples' attitude toward Lin Fan change at the end?
options: They started to fear and respect his strength.

L1.text:
Chapter 2: The School Test

Three days later, it is morning. There is a big bell sound in the city. Many people go to the Qingyun School. Today is the big test. It is a chance to change their lives.

Lin Fan wears a clean grey shirt. He looks like a poor worker. But inside his body, the stone ball is moving. It makes his body strong.

"Look! It is Lin Fan," a boy says. His name is Zhang Hu. He is mean to Lin Fan. Zhang Hu laughs. "You cannot do magic! Why are you here?"

Other people look at Lin Fan. They think he is a waste. Lin Fan does not say anything. He waits in line.

"Next, Lin Fan!" a teacher calls.

Lin Fan walks to a big black stone. It is very tall. The teacher says, "Put your hand on the stone."

Lin Fan puts his hand on the cold stone. Suddenly, the stone ball inside him spins fast. A lot of magic goes into the stone.

Vroom!

The stone shakes. A bright green light comes out. It is very beautiful and strong.

"What?!" the teacher jumps up. He is very surprised.

The light goes up and up. A gold line on the stone stops at 99%.

"He has a Wood Spirit Root," an old teacher says. His voice shakes. "But the magic is so pure! It is 99% pure!"

Zhang Hu's face turns white. He is very shocked. In this world, pure magic is very powerful. Lin Fan will be very strong.

Lin Fan takes a metal card. It says "Outer Student." He walks away. He is not a poor miner now. But he knows many people are watching him. Life will be hard.

quiz.question: What is happening at the Qingyun School?
What does Zhang Hu do when he sees Lin Fan?
What happens when Lin Fan touches the stone?
options: A bright green light comes out.

quiz.question: Is Lin Fan's magic pure?

L2.text:
Chapter 2: The Sect Selection

Three days later, a loud bell rang through the city. Thousands of people gathered at the foot of the mountain for the Qingyun Sect selection. For normal people, this was the only way to become powerful.

Lin Fan wore a simple grey shirt. He looked like the same poor miner from three days ago. However, the stone bead in his body was moving slowly. It was healing and strengthening his veins every second.

"Hey, isn't that 'Broken Veins' Lin Fan?" a sharp voice asked.

It was Zhang Hu, the son of the mine boss. He always treated Lin Fan badly. Zhang Hu laughed loudly. "Did you lose your mind in the mine? Someone with broken veins shouldn't be here!"

Other people look at Lin Fan. They think he is a waste. Lin Fan does not say anything. He waits in line.

"Next, Lin Fan!" a teacher calls.

Lin Fan walks to a big black stone. It is very tall. The teacher says, "Put your hand on the stone."

Lin Fan puts his hand on the cold stone. Suddenly, the stone ball inside him spins fast. A lot of magic goes into the stone.

Vroom!

The stone shakes. A bright green light comes out. It is very beautiful and strong.

"What?!" the teacher jumps up. He is very surprised.

The light goes up and up. A gold line on the stone stops at 99%.

"He has a Wood Spirit Root," an old teacher says. His voice shakes. "But the magic is so pure! It is 99% pure!"

Zhang Hu's face turns white. He is very shocked. In this world, pure magic is very powerful. Lin Fan will be very strong.

Lin Fan takes a metal card. It says "Outer Student." He walks away. He is not a poor miner now. But he knows many people are watching him. Life will be hard.

quiz.question: What is special about the Spirit Spring Valley?
options: The water has a lot of magic energy.
It is a very quiet place to sleep.

quiz.question: What did Wang Qian tell Lin Fan to do?
options: To go back to the mine.
To go and carry water buckets.

quiz.question: How did Lin Fan see the fireball?
options: It looked very slow because of his "Magic Sense."
He couldn't see it because it was too fast.

quiz.question: Why was Lin Fan's water arrow so strong?
options: Because the arrow was very big.
Because his magic was very pure.

L3.text:
Chapter 2: The Sect Selection

Three days later, the morning air was filled with the sound of a resonating bell. At the base of the mountain, the Qingyun Sect's massive square was crowded with thousands of candidates. For commoners, this was the "Dragon Gate"—the only way to rise above their low social class.

Lin Fan arrived in a faded grey shirt. He kept his head down, appearing like the same destitute miner he had always been. However, he could feel the stone bead in his dantian pulsing rhythmically, continuously nourishing his newly reborn meridians.

"Well, look who it is! The 'Broken Veins' Lin Fan!" a mocking voice cried out.

It was Zhang Hu, the son of the mine overseer. Zhang Hu had bullied Lin Fan for years. He stepped forward with his followers and laughed. "Did the dust in the mine ruin your brain? A person with broken veins participating in the selection? You must be dreaming!"

The crowd began to gossip, casting judgmental looks at Lin Fan. Some thought he was desperate, while others thought he was simply a fool. Lin Fan ignored the noise, his expression remaining as calm as a deep pool of water. This indifference irritated Zhang Hu. He leaned in and hissed, "Don't break the testing stone. You can't afford to pay for it!"

"Next, Lin Fan!" the sect officer announced.

Lin Fan stepped toward a massive black monument covered in ancient, mysterious patterns. "Place your hand on the surface and inject your Qi," the officer commanded without looking up.

The instant Lin Fan's palm touched the cold stone, the bead inside him sensed a resonance. It spun violently, and a surge of incredibly pure energy erupted from his arm and poured into the monument.

OOM—!

The silent monument began to tremble violently. A brilliant green glow emerged from the base and transformed into a dazzling pillar of light that pierced the sky.

"What is this?!" The officer leaped to his feet, accidentally snapping his brush in half. The noisy square suddenly fell into a deathly silence.

Within the blinding green light, a golden line climbed rapidly. It shot past the 50% and 80% marks, finally slamming into the 99% position near the very top!

"Wood Element Spirit Root," an elder announced from the high platform, his voice trembling with excitement. "However... the purity is 99%! He is almost a 'Flawless Body'!"

Zhang Hu's eyes nearly popped out of his head. His face turned from red to a ghostly pale. In the cultivation world, purity determined your combat power and the speed of your breakthroughs. 99% purity meant Lin Fan would be ten times stronger than average cultivators of the same rank.

Lin Fan reclaimed his hand as the light faded. The officer, with shaking hands, gave him a bronze "Outer Disciple" token. Under the gaze of thousands—some shocked, some jealous, and some greedy—Lin Fan walked away. He gripped the token tightly. He knew the humble miner was gone, but he also knew he was now at the center of a dangerous storm.

quiz.question: What does the term "Dragon Gate" symbolize in the text?
options: A literal gate made of dragons.
A rare opportunity to change one's destiny.

quiz.question: What was the hidden change in Lin Fan's body?
options: The stone bead was strengthening his meridians.

quiz.question: Why did the officer snap his brush?
options: He was angry at Lin Fan.
He was shocked by the sudden, intense pillar of light.
The brush was old and poor quality.

quiz.question: How does 99% purity affect a cultivator's future?
options: It makes them eat more food.
It significantly increases their training speed and power.
It means they can only use wood magic.

quiz.question: Which word best describes the crowd's reaction at the end of the chapter?

Lin Fan wore a simple grey shirt. He looked like the same poor miner from three days ago. However, the stone bead in his body was moving slowly. It was healing and strengthening his veins every second.

L3.text:
Chapter 3: The Spirit Spring争夺

The outer gate of Qingyun Sect was not just a starting point for cultivation, but a cold jungle.

After only a few days in the outer gate, Lin Fan deeply understood the survival law here: resources are always limited, and the only way to get resources is to fight.

On the west side of the outer gate, there was a "Spirit Spring Valley." The valley had dozens of hot spring eyes. The spiritual energy from these springs was several times richer than the outside world. It was a treasure place for disciples in the Qi-training period to break through bottlenecks. However, when Lin Fan stepped into the valley, he saw a strict hierarchy - each spring was occupied by a powerful senior disciple, and the weaker ones could only absorb some sparse spiritual energy at the valley entrance.

Lin Fan needed to break through the Qi-training Layer 1 as soon as possible. Only by truly entering the Qi-training period could the stone bead in his dantian play a greater role. He put his hands behind his back and scanned the valley. Finally, he stopped by a spring at the edge.

"Stop. This place is not for you," a gloomy voice said.

A young man in a dark blue robe with a strong body blocked Lin Fan. Behind him, Zhang Hu with a swollen and bruised face pointed at Lin Fan resentfully: "Cousin, that's him! He made my dad lose face at the selection. You must teach him a good lesson!"

This was Wang Qian, with three layers of Qi cultivation. He was quite famous in the outer gate.

Wang Qian looked at Lin Fan disdainfully, his tone full of arrogance: "I hear your purity is high? But in the world of cultivation, a genius who hasn't grown up is nothing. Newbie, this spring doesn't belong to you. Go carry water buckets behind the mountain. That's the work for a miner like you."

Before he finished, Wang Qian's right hand swung violently, his spiritual power fluctuated sharply.

"Fireball!"

A fireball the size of a fist, red and hot, condensed instantly. It came with a howling wind and hot temperature. It flew straight at Lin Fan's face. The surrounding disciples exclaimed. A fireball from someone at Qi Layer 3 could seriously injure a disciple who hadn't officially started cultivation.

However, facing the intense heat, Lin Fan didn't show any panic.

As the fireball approached, the stone bead in his body shook violently. A wonderful "spiritual sense" spread instantly. The originally fast fireball seemed to move slowly in Lin Fan's vision, as if stuck in mud. He could clearly see the uneven distribution of spiritual power inside the fireball and the slightly stagnant meridian flow when Wang Qian cast the spell.

"Too many weaknesses."

Lin Fan snorted coldly in his heart. He tapped the ground lightly with his toes, and his body moved strangely to the left. The hot fireball brushed past his afterimage and exploded on a rock behind him, leaving a black scorched hole.

"How could he dodge?" Wang Qian's pupils shrank.

Before he could cast another spell, Lin Fan had already acted. He didn't know any advanced fighting techniques. He simply followed the most basic "Water Control Technique" from the entry manual, guiding the purified spiritual power along the path of the stone bead.

"Water arrow, go!"

Lin Fan put his two fingers together and pointed at Wang Qian in the air. A light blue water vapor compressed instantly, turning into a crystal clear long arrow.

Although the water arrow looked thin, the spiritual power inside was extremely pure after being refined by the stone bead. It was as solid as reality.

Wang Qian sneered and quickly brought up a light yellow defensive shield: "Basic water control can't hurt me! Break!"

"Puff!"

A muffled sound.

The light blue water arrow didn't break as Wang Qian expected. Instead, it pierced through the yellow shield as easily as a hot knife through butter. It stopped accurately at Wang Qian's throat, and the sharp cold air gave him goosebumps.

The whole place was dead silent.

Wang Qian's forehead was covered with fine sweat. He knew that as long as Lin Fan wished, the arrow could take his life instantly.

"Qi Layer 3, that's all," Lin Fan withdrew his fingers, and the water arrow turned back into fog and disappeared.

He ignored the ashen-faced Wang Qian and Zhang Hu, and walked straight to the spring. He sat down cross-legged. The other disciples made way for him, their eyes full of unprecedented fear.

This was Lin Fan's first fight in the outer gate. He proved with strength that even basic spells could have power beyond their level when faced with ultimate purity. He also knew very well that this was just the beginning of this cruel jungle.

quiz.question: What is the "Law of the Jungle" mentioned in the story?
options: Only the strongest disciples get the best resources.
Everyone must live in the forest.

quiz.question: Why was Zhang Hu present at the Spirit Spring?
options: To practice magic with Lin Fan.
To ask his cousin, Wang Qian, to bully Lin Fan.

quiz.question: What enabled Lin Fan to dodge the fireball so easily?
options: The stone bead gave him a "spiritual sense" that slowed down his perception of the attack.

quiz.question: What happened to Wang Qian's earth shield?
options: It blocked the water arrow successfully.
It was easily pierced by Lin Fan's high-purity water arrow.

quiz.question: How did the other disciples' attitude toward Lin Fan change at the end?
options: They started to fear and respect his strength.

dictionary.Spirit Root: 灵根，感应和吸收灵气的体质
dictionary.Withered Veins: 废脉，经脉闭塞无法修炼
dictionary.Qi-training: 练气期，修炼第一个阶段
dictionary.Spiritual Purity: 灵气纯度，灵力纯净程度
dictionary.Thick Soil Longevity Skill: 厚土长生功，防御型功法
